title: New-device recovery flows from wallet-derived keys through the Swarm user manifest.
content:
## Claim
A user's drives can be recovered on any device purely from wallet + Swarm — no server handoff.

## Flow
1. User opens Connect on a new device (served from Swarm).
2. Signs in with wallet → derives encryption keys.
3. Plugin reads the user manifest from the Swarm feed.
4. Registers Swarm remotes for each discovered drive.
5. `SwarmChannel.inbox` pulls all operations from per-document feeds.
6. SyncManager applies them to the reactor → drive materializes in PGlite.
7. Cursor is set; future syncs are incremental.

## Why this works end-to-end
- The wallet signature is deterministic — same wallet yields the same keys and same feed topics.
- The user manifest is the single discovery root; from it the plugin walks drives → documents → batches.
- No centralized account state is needed on any server.

## Source
Swarm Channel Architecture, "Flow 3: New Device Recovery (from Swarm)".
description: On a new device, signing with the wallet derives keys, the plugin reads the user manifest, registers Swarm remotes per drive, and SyncManager rehydrates PGlite.
